summary: Small, precise byte-match PR: GLM-5.2 (via the diff-aware repair loop) isolated a single commutative operand-order mismatch (add r5,r6,r8 vs add r5,r8,r6) in fn_80017990's compare-load, and the fix rewrote the array-index expression as pointer-arithmetic to force base-first codegen. Also added a named SummaryPageEntry accessor layer over lbl_80266918 so the byte-match source no longer exposes raw 0x4C offset arithmetic in the function bodies.
decomp_lessons: MWCC operand order for a commutative add depends on source spelling: '*(s32*)(&arr[idx*SZ+OFF])' (address-of-array-element) lowers index-first; '*(s32*)((u8*)arr + idx*SZ + OFF)' (pointer-arithmetic) lowers base-first. Swapping forms is a legitimate lever for commutative-operand-order mismatches beyond the plain add/sub case.

Directly indexing a typed array (SummaryPageEntry arr[idx]) instead of raw byte-offset pointer math changed MWCC register allocation and regressed the match (99.67742% -> 80.6452%) -- proof that readability refactors must be verified per-function, not assumed safe just because the struct shape is correct.
A volatile-qualified typed table view also regressed the match (to 73.5294%, with added instructions) -- volatile changes MWCC codegen shape, not just semantics.
This PR explicitly documents itself as byte-match evidence, not final PC-port-ready source: the accessor macros still centralize MWCC-sensitive address spelling as portability debt to resolve later.
smart_moves: GLM-5.2 succeeded where deepseek-v4-pro, kimi-k2.7-code, and mimo-v2.5-pro all stalled at baseline (99.67742%) in the diff-aware repair loop -- confirms GLM-5.2 as the primary 'cracker' model for single-instruction diff-repair tasks per the 2026-06-15 benchmark.
Recorded the win to the knowledge graph via bestof.py with lever tag 'commutative-operand-order' so future agents can search for this exact technique.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Lever: commutative-operand-order. Rewrite '&arr[idx*SZ+OFF]' as '(u8*)arr + idx*SZ + OFF' to flip which operand MWCC emits first in a commutative add, without changing the +OFF load displacement.
SUMMARY_PAGE_ENTRY_AT / SUMMARY_PAGE_DISPLAY_COLOR macros wrap the sensitive byte-offset math so callers read as typed accessors while preserving the exact matching codegen underneath.
naming_conventions: SummaryPageEntry struct (0x4C bytes) for lbl_80266918 entries: displayColor[3], dataSource, gaugeMatchId, drawHandler, messageId fields identified from access patterns.
review_feedback: Rejected: direct SummaryPageEntry array indexing (regressed fn_80017990 to 80.6452%).
Rejected: volatile typed table view (regressed to 73.5294%, added instructions).

key_files.role: fn_80017990 restored to 100% byte-match via base-first pointer-arithmetic compare load; SummaryPageEntry typed accessor macros added for lbl_80266918.
GLM-5.2's winning one-line diff-repair candidate, recorded as KG evidence.
Benchmark note documenting the model comparison and the exact instruction-level wall that was cracked.
